front: Is an LLM stateful or stateless?
back: Stateless - it retains nothing between requests. Every apparent memory is text placed into its context window by the app.
Layer 1: the fixed-size whiteboard of text the model sees for one request. On the whiteboard = known; off it = nonexistent.
Layer 2: the app re-sends the whole conversation with each message. The model re-reads everything, every time - memory as re-reading.
Layer 3: facts the app extracts ('user is vegetarian'), stores in a database, and re-inserts into future chats. Sticky notes on the monitor.
Layer 4: large document collections stay outside the model; relevant passages are searched out and inserted just in time (the basis of RAG).

front: Why does AI forget the start of long chats?
back: The conversation outgrew the context window, so the oldest messages were dropped or summarized away.

front: Why does a new chat know nothing about yesterday's chat?
back: Chat history belongs to one conversation. Only saved memory (extracted facts) crosses between chats.
